claims.claim: As of May 2026 the Israeli personal-import VAT/customs exemption threshold is USD 130 (product value, excluding shipping and insurance when itemized separately).
claims.source_quote: מ-25.02.2026 משלוח עם מוצרים שערכם הכולל עד 130 דולר פטור לחלוטין ממסי יבוא

claims.claim: The Knesset revoked the December 2025 order that raised the threshold from USD 75 to USD 150 on 24 February 2026 (vote 59-25).
claims.source_quote: המרד בליכוד: בוטל צו הפטור ממע"מ עד 150 דולר

claims.claim: Within 24 hours of the Knesset revocation, the Finance Minister signed a new order setting the threshold to USD 130, effective midnight 24-25 February 2026.
claims.source_quote: סמוטריץ' חתם על צו חדש לפטור ממע"מ על יבוא מחו"ל עד 130 דולר

claims.claim: The personal-import exemption does NOT apply to tobacco, electronic cigarettes, alcohol, or alcoholic beverages; these are taxed in full regardless of value.
claims.source_quote: excluding tobacco, alcohol, and certain other goods

claims.claim: Israel's standard VAT rate is 18% (raised from 17% effective 1 January 2025).
claims.source_quote: The 2025 current rate of VAT is 18%

claims.claim: The VAT rate increase from 17% to 18% was approved by the Knesset plenum and entered into force on 1 January 2025.
claims.source_quote: Knesset Plenum approves order raising VAT rate from 17% to 18%

claims.claim: Personal imports valued between USD 130 and USD 500 owe VAT 18% only; customs duty is waived under the personal-import regime.
claims.source_quote: מוצרים שעלו בין 130 דולר ל-500 דולר חייבים במע"מ בלבד - 18%

claims.claim: Personal imports valued above USD 500 owe full customs duty, VAT 18%, and purchase tax where applicable.
claims.source_quote: מוצרים שעלו מעל 500 דולר חייבים בתשלום מע"מ וחלקם חייבים גם בתשלום מכס ומס קנייה

claims.claim: Shipments valued above USD 1,000 are treated as commercial regardless of who is buying, requiring a customs broker and a full import declaration (rashimon).
claims.source_quote: מוצרים שעלו 1,000-500 דולר חייבים בתשלום מע"מ וחלקם חייבים גם בתשלום מכס ומס קנייה

claims.claim: Shaar Olami is the official Israeli customs tariff query portal, hosted at customs.mof.gov.il and operated by the Israel Tax Authority.
claims.source_quote: Reservations to use of the Customs and Purchase Tax Tariff

claims.claim: The official Personal Import Tax calculator is available at shaarolami-query.customs.mof.gov.il/CustomspilotWeb/PersonalImportTax.
claims.source_quote: מחשבון מסי יבוא - רשות המסים בישראל

claims.claim: Israel uses the international Harmonized System at 6 digits and adds 2 Israel-specific digits (positions 7-8) for local duty and purchase-tax rules.
claims.source_quote: the Customs and Purchase Tax Tariff

claims.claim: The US-Israel FTA (signed 1985, first US bilateral FTA) requires US content to be at least 35% of the appraised value to qualify for preferential duty.
The modernized Canada-Israel FTA (CIFTA) entered into force on 1 September 2019; origin proof is Form B239.
claims.source_quote: Memorandum D11-5-6 - Rules of origin under the Canada-Israel Free Trade Agreement

claims.claim: EU-Israel preferential trade uses the Pan-Euro-Mediterranean (PEM) Convention 2012 rules; Israel has not ratified the revised PEM Convention.
claims.source_quote: EU-Israel trade relationship and Association Agreement

claims.claim: Israeli Customs requires a wet-ink original signature on EUR.1 movement certificates and does not accept electronically signed EUR.1 documents.
The Bank of Israel publishes the daily representative exchange rates (USD, EUR, GBP to ILS) used for customs valuation.
Shipping and insurance are excluded from the personal-import threshold test as long as they are itemized separately on the invoice; if bundled into the product price, the combined figure is tested.
claims.source_quote: excluding shipping and insurance costs, where calculated separately
